text.title: aws-iam-practical-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for aws-iam-practical-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using Action: "*" with Resource: "*" in any application workload policy (the "AdministratorAccess" pattern for a service).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Grants the workload the ability to create IAM users, modify billing, and delete any resource. Compromise of the credential gives attacker full account access.

failure-mode.description: Storing access keys on EC2 instances via environment variables or ~/.aws/credentials.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Keys never auto-rotate, survive AMI copies, and are accessible to any code running on the instance including injected code. Use instance roles with IMDS instead.
